steps.title: Express x in terms of y from the first equation
steps.content:
The first equation $\frac{x}{y} = 3$ implies $x = 3y$.

This substitution simplifies the second equation by eliminating a variable.

steps.title: Substitute x into the second equation
steps.content:
Replace $x$ with $3y$ in $\frac{2x}{ty} = 18$:
$$\frac{2(3y)}{ty} = 18$$
Simplify numerator and cancel $y$ (since $y \ne 0$):
$$\frac{6}{t} = 18$$

steps.title: Solve for t
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $t$ and divide by 18:
$$6 = 18t$$
$$t = \frac{6}{18} = \frac{1}{3}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
